effect.content: YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.

quotes: YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.
YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.

quotes: YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.

rules.intent: Maintain consistent, approved campaign messaging for the product launch.
rules.rule_text: The primary campaign headline must appear verbatim as 'YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.'

effect.content: LISRAYA is a once-daily FDA-approved pill that blocks the specific inflammatory signals that drive dermatomyositis, so you can: *Increase your muscle strength* / *Improve your skin symptoms* / *Rely less on steroids* / *Do more of the activities you love*.

quotes: "LISRAYA is a once-daily FDA-approved pill that blocks the specific inflammatory signals that drive dermatomyositis, so you can:" → bullets: *Increase your muscle strength* / *Improve your skin symptoms* / *Rely less on steroids* / *Do more of the activities you love*.

"LISRAYA is a once-daily FDA-approved pill that blocks the specific inflammatory signals that drive dermatomyositis, so you can:" → bullets: *Increase your muscle strength* / *Improve your skin symptoms* / *Rely less on steroids* / *Do more of the activities you love*.

rules.intent: Ensure accurate, FDA-aligned presentation of patient-facing efficacy points.
rules.rule_text: Product benefits must utilize the approved introductory phrase and its four associated bulleted items: 'Increase your muscle strength', 'Improve your skin symptoms', 'Rely less on steroids', and 'Do more of the activities you love'.

quotes: Unlike other treatments for dermatomyositis (DM), which suppress the immune system generally, LISRAYA targets the root cause of the disease.
...LISRAYA works by turning off the specific inflammatory switches that are stuck in the 'on' position.

rules.intent: Maintain scientific accuracy when explaining the targeted action of LISRAYA.
rules.rule_text: Mechanism of Action (MOA) copy must align to either the primary framing ('Unlike other treatments for dermatomyositis (DM), which suppress the immune system generally, LISRAYA targets the root cause of the disease.') or the alternative switch analogy ('...LISRAYA works by turning off the specific inflammatory switches that are stuck in the "on" position.').

effect.content: LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.

quotes: LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.
LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.

quotes: LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.

rules.intent: Clearly differentiate LISRAYA from competitive biologics requiring injection/infusion.
rules.rule_text: Administration benefits must express convenient once-daily oral dosing verbatim: 'LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.'

rules.intent: Encourage patient-physician dialogue using approved regulatory parameters.
rules.rule_text: The patient-targeted doctor discussion call to action must use the exact wording: 'Talk to your doctor about LISRAYA, the DM treatment you may be looking for.'

rules.intent: Maintain brand alignment and trademark integrity for program communications.
rules.rule_text: The patient support program must always be referenced by its official trademarked name: 'My Compass Support' (providing Personalized Communication via PAL — text, phone, email, in person; and Authorization Support).
